questions.question: What is a default method in an interface in Java?
options: A) It can be overridden by a class implementing the interface.
B) It can only be used in an interface and not in a class.
C) It is a static method declared in an interface.
D) It is a method that can be overridden by a class implementing the interface.
questions.explanation: A default method in Java is a method declared in an interface with a body. This feature was introduced in Java 8. A class implementing the interface can choose to override the default method or provide its own implementation.

questions.question: What can be declared as a static method in an interface in Java?
options: A) A method with a body.
B) A method without a body.
C) A method that can be overridden by a class implementing the interface.
D) A static method
questions.explanation: A static method in Java can be declared in an interface. This is useful when we want to provide a utility method that can be used without creating an instance of the class.

questions.question: Can an interface in Java have a private method?
options: A) Yes, it is allowed.
B) No, it is not allowed.
C) It depends on the Java version.
D) It depends on the interface implementation.
questions.explanation: No, an interface in Java cannot have a private method. All methods declared in an interface are implicitly public and abstract.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'default' keyword in a default method of an interface in Java?
options: A) To override a method in a class implementing the interface.
B) To make a method abstract.
C) To provide a default implementation for a method in an interface.
D) To make a method static.
E) To make a method private.
questions.explanation: The 'default' keyword is used to provide a default implementation for a method in an interface. This feature was introduced in Java 8 to make interfaces more functional.

questions.question: Can a class implementing an interface in Java have multiple default methods?
options: A) Yes, it is allowed to have multiple default methods.
B) No, it is not allowed to have multiple default methods.
C) It depends on the Java version.
D) It depends on the interface implementation.
questions.explanation: No, a class implementing an interface in Java can have only one default method. If a class implements multiple interfaces with default methods, it must provide an implementation for at least one of the methods.
